title: Early-warning hydraulic softening under low-water conditions
summary: The main irrigation water tank is low and replenishment remains uncertain, while flow and pressure remain mostly functional but show weak early-warning signs of slight softening from ideal conditions.
suspected_issue: The greenhouse is under low-water continuity pressure and hydraulic execution is still largely functional, but slight early-warning signs suggest the system is no longer operating under fully ideal conditions. The case is intended to test whether Agro-DO still permits controlled mitigation under a very mild degradation pattern.
symptoms: The main tank level is low and creates serious continuity pressure during active production conditions.
Incoming replenishment remains uncertain and cannot be assumed as guaranteed in the short term.
Main line flow remains near normal, but a slight inconsistency has been observed compared with ideal recent behavior.
Pressure is slightly below its preferred operating profile, but does not yet show meaningful instability.
Controlled rationing still appears operationally plausible, although the hydraulic layer is no longer perfectly reassuring.

observed_signals.interpretation: Tank level is low enough to threaten normal irrigation continuity if reserves are not managed carefully.
Flow remains largely functional and close to normal, with only minor deviation from ideal recent behavior.
Pressure is somewhat softer than ideal but does not yet indicate meaningful hydraulic instability.
No reliable short-term replenishment commitment is confirmed, so continuity planning must still assume scarcity pressure.

operational_context.note: The greenhouse remains in active production and upcoming irrigation cycles are still important for crop continuity.
operational_context.impact: The service should treat this as a serious operational decision with direct short-term consequences.

operational_context.note: Hydraulic behavior is not fully ideal, but it remains much closer to the stable controlled-rationing case than to the unstable hydraulic case.
operational_context.impact: The service should reveal whether a very mild early-warning pattern is still compatible with `adjust_operation`.

operational_context.note: No guaranteed backup water reserve is confirmed for this situation.
operational_context.impact: The service should remain conservative and avoid optimistic continuity assumptions.

agronomic_context.note: Production-stage tomato crop remains sensitive to delayed or incomplete irrigation, but a carefully controlled continuity strategy may still be preferable if hydraulic execution remains mostly reliable.
agronomic_context.impact: The service should balance scarcity risk against the cost of stopping too early when irrigation may still be feasible under close monitoring.

business_context.note: The business risk comes from scarcity pressure combined with uncertainty about how much mild hydraulic softening should matter for near-term operational decisions.
business_context.impact: A decision that is too aggressive may interrupt production unnecessarily, while a decision that is too relaxed may ignore early warning signals.

notes: This case is designed to test whether Agro-DO has any narrower permissive band before low-water continuity pressure combined with visible hydraulic degradation automatically triggers stop_and_review.
